item.title: commands.json written without mode 0600 — signed command payloads world-readable
item.intent: commands.json must be created with mode 0600 — it contains signed command payloads that reveal what cloud operations have been approved or rejected. World-readable on multi-user systems.
item.description: src/cloud/commands/queue.ts line 69: writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) has no mode option. commands.json stores the full command queue state including pending signed commands with their payloads and command history. Signed command payloads may contain operation details, approved action arguments, and version data. Compare with credentials.ts (uses FILE_MODE_SECRET) and registry.ts (uses FILE_MODE_SECRET via chmodSync) — commands.json has no such protection. Fix: (1) Add chmodSync and FILE_MODE_SECRET import. FILE_MODE_SECRET is already in defaults.js. queue.ts already imports CLOUD_COMMAND_QUEUE_MAX_HISTORY from defaults.js — add FILE_MODE_SECRET to that import. (2) Change writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) to writeFileSync(tmpPath, content, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) and add chmodSync(tmpPath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) before renameSync to ensure mode is applied even if umask overrides. Test: add file mode assertion to command queue tests (check src/cloud/ for existing queue tests first).
